_description: Phase 5 Azure-Lily DIMM-top residual cycle deltas between the Regime-A analytical sim (gemm_dsp with DSP_WDITH=4) and the cycle-accurate RTL, annotated with file:line root causes. A per-stage delta present here is accepted by run_checks.py (DIMM latency path) as long as |delta - expected_delta_cycles| <= expected_delta_tolerance.

_schema.delta_cycles: RTL - sim (negative = RTL faster than sim, positive = RTL slower = conservative sim)
_schema.root_cause: file:line citation of the structural or FSM-handshake source of the residual

note: Phase 6A (2026-04-23) — Azure-Lily dsp_mac 4-wide fix. Pre-Phase-6A the generator emitted dsp_mac with K=ceil(d_head/EPW=5)=13 (mac_qk) and K=ceil(N/EPW=5)=26 (mac_sv) while the dsp_mac body only multiplied 4 int8 pairs/cycle (bytes 0..3 of the 40-bit bus). That K-sizing bug made the RTL look ~19% faster than the sim's gemm_dsp (DSP_WDITH=4; azurelily/IMC/peripherals/fpga_fabric.py:181), producing a structural -132 cyc E2E delta (-1 score / -126 softmax / -6 wsum). Phase 6A re-sizes K to ceil(d/EPW_DSP=4)=16 (mac_qk) and ceil(N/EPW_DSP=4)=32 (mac_sv) (nl_dpe/gen_dimm_azurelily_top.py::_gen_dimm_top_azurelily), and updates the sim extraction (gen_expected_cycles.py) to fold the 2-cyc per-row SRAM-prime setup into k_tile_qk. Post-Phase-6A all residuals drop to 0..+1 cyc (pure modelling granularity). All four entries are modelling_granularity, none structural.

deltas.root_cause: Exact match after Phase 6A sim extraction update. The sim extraction now adds a 2-cyc per-row SRAM-prime setup (feed_setup_per_row=2 in gen_expected_cycles.py), matching the RTL's dsp_mac.valid gating on mac_count>=2 (azurelily_dimm_top_d64_c128.v:107). Sim = k_tile_qk + 2 = 16 + 2 = 18; RTL also = 18.

1-cyc boundary rounding on the softmax probe's first S_NORM output (clb_softmax @ azurelily_dimm_top_d64_c128.v:287-294). Sim formula = (N-1) * (k_tile_qk + feed_setup) + S_INV + first_S_NORM = 127 * 18 + 1 + 1 = 2288 cyc; RTL measured 2289 cyc (1-cyc NBA-commit on the probe latch).

Exact match: RTL dsp_mac with K=ceil(N/EPW_DSP=4)=32 (azurelily_dimm_top_d64_c128.v:125) produces first out_valid after 32 valid cycles; sim k_tile_sv = ceil(N/DSP_WDITH=4) = 32 (azurelily/IMC/peripherals/fpga_fabric.py:181). No setup-gating on mac_sv (valid=attn_valid directly from softmax), so no 2-cyc SRAM-prime offset applies here.

Additive sum of per-stage residuals: score (0) + softmax (+1) + wsum (0) + ~1 cyc probe latch rounding = +2 cyc. Classification modelling_granularity. For the paper the Azure-Lily RTL cycles match the sim prediction within 0.1% on end-to-end DIMM latency at N=128, d=64 — near-perfect parity with the NL-DPE's Phase 3+4 +34 cyc residual (both architectures now within FSM handshake granularity of their respective sims).
